model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Duke is the #1 overall seed with elite metrics (KenPom/Torvik rank #1) and a 31-2 record, featuring top-4 offense and #1 defense. While Kansas has strong defensive efficiency (rank 10) and tournament experience under Bill Self, Duke's overall talent depth and Cameron Boozer's star power should overcome the Jayhawks' challenges. Duke's injuries to Caleb Foster and Patrick Ngongba II are concerning, but their superior analytics and ability to win close games throughout the ACC season give them the edge in this Sweet 16 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Duke vs Kansas Sweet 16 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke holds significant analytical advantages despite injury concerns, while Kansas brings strong defense and tournament pedigree.
reasoningStep.evidence: Duke ranks #1 in both KenPom and Torvik with 31-2 record, elite offense (rank 4) and defense (rank 1). Kansas ranks #21-22 with 23-10 record, strong defense (rank 10) but weaker offense (rank 56-57). Duke is missing Caleb Foster (foot fracture) and Patrick Ngongba II, but has shown ability to win without key players. Kansas has tournament experience under Bill Self but faces massive talent disparity. Duke's overall superiority and star power with Cameron Boozer should prevail despite injury concerns.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
